summary: Iteration 130: Pastry module and EchoCanvasStudio integration testing. Fixed JSX syntax error in ChefGioTraining/index.tsx. Pastry module loads with 3 navigation sections. Echo Canvas tabs (Canvas Design, Cake Builder, Templates) are functional. Three.js v0.184.0 is installed.

minor.issue: Returns 'Menu not found' - expected when no templates exist

integration_issues.issue: Editor content area appears mostly empty/black - lazy loading may need more time or there may be rendering issues

action_items: Canvas Design editor content area appears empty - may need investigation if editor components are rendering correctly
Chef Gio Training panel not found in sidebar navigation - may be under different section or renamed
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: JSX syntax error in /app/client/modules/ChefGioTraining/index.tsx - added missing closing tag at line 371

success_rate.frontend: 85% - Pastry module loads, tabs work, but Canvas editor content needs verification

verified_features.FRONTEND_pastry_module_opens: ✓ Pastry module opens from sidebar under CULINARY OPERATIONS
verified_features.FRONTEND_pastry_3_sections: ✓ Shows 3 navigation sections: RECIPES, PASTRY STUDIO, OPERATIONS
verified_features.FRONTEND_templates_tab: ✓ pastry-canvas-tab-templates shows templates gallery with 'No saved templates yet' message

context_for_next_testing_agent: Pastry module integration with EchoCanvasStudio is working. The 3 tabs (Canvas Design, Cake Builder, Templates) are accessible via data-testid selectors. Canvas Design editor shows a toolbar on the left but main content area appears dark - may need to verify if the full editor is rendering. Three.js is installed. Fixed JSX syntax error in ChefGioTraining/index.tsx that was causing app to fail to load.

bug_fix_applied.issue: JSX syntax error - missing closing tag causing 'Unexpected token' error
bug_fix_applied.fix: Added missing tag at line 371 to properly close the else branch container div
